Hey Alma,

I see you mentioned the Federal Funds rate this morning. Do you think the next cut would come on market strength or weakness? If we get that 10% unwinding like you’re talking about, I’d assume that may trigger a FED cut in May. And are you assuming a FED cut as good news or bad news? If you look back historically, cuts tend to start happening at the beginning of economic weakness.

comment.body: Sometimes I just don’t get people. We’re here on the Substack of one of the most respected quant analysts..for literally pennies. This is the woman who was attacked for like a a year by jealous haters, shrugged off every bit of it, predicted every market trend with incredible accuracy, and is admired by institutional players, data providers, and more. People begged her for a year to start a Substack and teach them, and now here we are, getting insight into her thinking, and while she’s made it clear that she focuses mainly on SPX and VIX, and the most important question people can come up with is her opinion on some random stock or ponzi crap. Unbelievable.

comment.body:
Well, Alma never actually said that. What she said, exactly, was that the market is pricing in volatility expansion, but don’t forget that volatility works in both directions. She also mentioned that it’s a good setup for reversion trades.
Look, on both days so far, we’ve seen theta decay and vol decay. You can't win on 0DTE without volatility. The skew points to a volatility increase on Thursday, but if you read today’s post, it says that above 6150, it's a spot up/vol up scenario.
Be careful not to twist her words.

comment.body:
No, that’s not what she said. Reversion trade, guys.
If you actually read what she wrote, she predicted vol suppression, which means there’s no intraday edge. On the long side, you can’t profit from 0DTEs without volatility.
However, she did say that by Thursday, volatility could pick up a bit, which would favor reversion trading. But it’s not a guarantee..that’s why she keeps saying read the tape  day by day
